title: Burrow and Luna and the Old Secret

pageTimings.text:
Burrow was digging a new tunnel.
He dug every morning.
He liked the quiet of it.
He liked the dark of it.

Then his tunnel fell open.
He tumbled into a room.
It was large.
It was old.
It was not a tunnel.

The room had carvings on every wall.
It had small glowing stones in the ceiling.
It smelled of old wood and old magic.
Burrow had never seen it before.

Burrow went up to find Luna.
"Come with me," he said.
"Bring a light."
Luna said nothing.
She came.

Luna was very large for a tunnel.
She came anyway.
She did not say anything about this.

Luna stood in the doorway.
She was very still.
Her eyes went wide.
Then soft.
"Oh," said Luna.
Very quietly.

Luna walked slowly around the room.
She touched the carvings.
They lit up where she touched.
Blue and gold.
She had been here before.

"Have you been here before?" asked Burrow.
"Yes," said Luna.
She looked around the room.
"A very long time ago."
"What is it?" asked Burrow.

"This is the Memory Room," said Luna.
"This is where Moonwood keeps its oldest stories."
Burrow looked at the carvings.
"All of them?" he asked.
"All of them," said Luna.

Luna settled on a ledge.
She folded her wings.
She looked at the carvings for a moment.
"The first story of Moonwood," she began,
"is this."

Burrow sat very still and listened.
The room grew warmer.
The carvings lit up one by one.
Luna's voice was quiet and certain.
She had always known these stories.

It was very late when Burrow noticed.
"Will you tell me the rest?" he asked.
"Yes," said Luna.
"There is quite a lot."
They both settled in.
